title: English Thinking
objective: Students will explore english thinking concepts including Reviewing Own Writing, Knowing What You Don't Know.

steps.content: Let's explore Reviewing Own Writing! Have you ever wondered about this?
Evaluate whether your own writing achieves the effect you intended on a reader — go beyond checking for correctness to asking whether it actually works
Let me show you something cool. writing self-evaluation research grades 3-5
After you writes something — a story, a letter, a persuasive piece — do they think about whether it would have the effect they wanted on a reader, not just whether the spelling is right?
Great job! Today we learned about Reviewing Own Writing. writing self-evaluation research grades 3-5 metacognitive awareness of writing (Frontiers 2025)
Let's discover the amazing world of Knowing What You Don't Know!
Monitor your own vocabulary gaps — notice words you half-know, distinguish confident from uncertain knowledge, and develop strategies to resolve the uncertainty
Time to try this out: Noticing Unfamiliar Words Assessment research (grade 2+)
When you comes across a word they half-know — where they can guess the meaning but aren't sure — do they notice that gap and do something about it, like looking it up or asking?
Great job! Today we learned about Knowing What You Don't Know. Noticing Unfamiliar Words Assessment research (grade 2+) word consciousness and vocabulary metacognition research
